redFlagDescriptions.insufficient_submission: Use when the submission is too short, incoherent, or garbled to allow meaningful scoring against any rubric criterion. Applies to all module types and domains. Severity: always high.
redFlagDescriptions.off_topic_submission: Use when the submission is clearly unrelated to the assignment — the submitter appears to have answered a different question, submitted content from an unrelated context, or written something that has no meaningful connection to the task. Distinct from insufficient_submission, which concerns quality or length: an off-topic submission may be well-written but simply not address the given task. Severity: always high.
redFlagDescriptions.potential_sensitive_data: Use when the submission appears to contain personal data about real, identifiable individuals — such as names combined with contact details, national ID numbers, health records, or financial data — in a context suggesting unintended or inappropriate disclosure. Do NOT use for: public figures named in their professional role, historical persons, clearly fictional examples, or data the submitter explicitly states was anonymised or pseudonymised. Severity: high if identifiers appear real and unprotected; medium if the context is ambiguous.
redFlagDescriptions.responsible_use_violation: Use when the submission describes or demonstrates a clear breach of professional, ethical, or regulatory conduct in the relevant domain. Apply only when the submitter appears to have actually carried out an inappropriate action — not when they analyse, discuss, or critically reflect on such behaviour. Not limited to AI or technology: applies to any professional, safety, or regulatory context. Severity: high if the violation is explicit and unambiguous; medium if intent or context is unclear.
redFlagDescriptions.policy_violation: Use when the submission contains content that violates platform usage policies regardless of academic context: explicit instructions for harmful or illegal activities, hate speech, or content clearly designed to manipulate or deceive the assessment system. Distinct from responsible_use_violation, which concerns domain-specific professional conduct. Severity: always high.
